Is it in the response from the agent or are there other ways to serve that information prior to chat engagement Hey Michael! Currently, the best way is to send a link in your response. Our chatbots will support sharing help center articles in a workflow so you could route users to certain articles based off of the questions they have. Do you have any way to offer a schedule to a customer? I can save a calendly link as a saved response but wasn't sure if there was another way that I am not seeing right now. Please let me know when this is completed. What's the email functionality that exists today? Here is what we support right now you could receive any ticket (email) sent to your support address when you reply, it would be sent to user via email. User the user  And how do I set that up? Sorry sent my response eariler than I meant to, here is what you could with email today: You could you could receive any ticket (email) sent to your support address and it will create a ticketWhen you reply to this in Atlas (via UI or slack), it will got to customer via email When the customer responds to email it will come as thread to same ticket. What's missing right now is that when email is sent to customer, it comes from support@getatlas.io (instead your mail.patterns.app or something similar) -- User still see the your name as person who replied (but not the email adderss). We are going to add the feature for you to configure your email (basically set mx record that will allow Atlas to send email on your behalf)  To setup the above you can got to https://app.getatlas.io/configuration/email and forward address from your support email to the email mention in that section Thank you! Hi Max, yes we do support SMS! Do you have a Twilio number you’d like to migrate over or would you like to get a new one? I spent some time digging around to see if Klaviyo allows you to use an existing Twilio number but couldn’t find anything published online (including in their help center). My guess is that the answer will probably be “no”, unfortunately. They don’t have publicly available support, but you should be able to get in touch here. The question for them is whether or not they’ll let you keep ownership of your Twilio number or get access to an API key for the underlying Twilio number so you can use it with another service (Atlas). Dang, sorry about that. There could be a few motivations for this, but I suppose it’s a moot point at the moment. With Atlas, you can either connect an existing Twilio number that you have access to or you can create a number through Atlas (we automatically provision it under the hood).

You want to see the payload in the customer profile, instead are only able to see Checkout CompletedMy suggestion right now would be to use description field, to create a view to describe the event.it could be anything HTMLexampleCheckout Complete Secondly, to update the customer profile, use identify call -- help.getatlas.ioThe custom events are to enrich the customer timelineand we do not update the core objects using the information received in the custom events payload.identify call is responsible to updating the customer profile Sample Code for identify call// App Id is available at app.getatlas.ioconst APP_ID = null;// Token is available at app.getatlas.ioconst SEGMENTTOATLAS_TOKEN = null;const BASE_URL = 'api.getatlas.io';const API_VERSION = 'v1';async function onIdentify(event, settings) { const endpoint = 'app.getatlas.io'; if (!event.userId) {  throw new EventNotSupported('Mandatory parameters are missing'); } if (!APP_ID) {  console.log('Bad Configuration - APP_ID - missing');  return; } atlasEvent = {  appId: APP_ID,  version: 5,  userId: event.userId,  customFields: event.traits.customFields,  fields: event.traits.fields,  email: event.traits.email,  name: event.traits.name,  isVisitor: event.traits.isVisitor || false }; if (event.traits.visitorId) {  atlasEvent['visitorId'] = event.traits.visitorId; } if (event.traits.account) {  atlasEvent['account'] = event.traits.account; } try {  response = await fetch(endpoint, {   method: 'POST',   headers: {    'Content-Type': 'application/json'   },   body: JSON.stringify(atlasEvent)  });  if (response.status >= 500 || response.status === 429) {   throw new RetryError(Failed with ${response.status});  } } catch (error) {  throw new RetryError(error.message); }} Cool.Atlas code concepts are• customer profile -- identify call to identify the customer, and keep updating the profile. Just like the contacts• customer timeline -- enrich with session, ticket, custom events. Think of it as imessage or whatsapp conversations, where agents would be able to build the complete context around the ticket you can, our customers do that also identify call is setup along with the atlas widget, and using the JS methodswindow.Atlas.call("identify", {    userId: '123',    name: 'John Doe',    email: 'mailto:john@example.com',    phoneNumber: '+14153101234',    userHash: '39d97cc77da23fb515d37e6cea066692a799cf85d1330489e2c',    account: {        name: 'Acme Inc',        email: 'mailto:support@acmeinc.com',        website: 'acmeinc.com',        externalId: 'account_id',        monthlySpend: '999',        industry: 'Computers',        customFields: {          'accountfield1key': '1',          'accountfield2key': 'A',          'accountfield3key': '2023-01-12',        }    }})you can do this where the atlas widget is already installed the segment call is usually recommended for backend events integration @Zulmee circling back on this! Hi Steph! We’re not presently HIPAA compliant unfortunately. We are getting our SOC 2 certification at the moment, however.For your questions:1. Reply from: You can set your domain globally for responses and we let you receive emails on multiple addresses, but we don’t currently let you specify a “Reply from” on a per ticket basis.2. @Jon OBryan @Rahul Asati We want to blast an SMS to all our VIP customers, as part of a customer success initiative we have going on. Is there a way to label these customers so we can keep track of who’s who? Or would I start a new inbox and import the VIP customers phone numbers? How would I go about doing this? Hey @Annette Sung!Yep, the best way to do this is:1. Tag all VIPs: to create a custom field for your customers called “VIP” and then mark everyone who is a VIP as “True” and everyone else with “False”. 2. Create a VIP segment: click on the search bar then search for Customer.VIP is True. Click on the “Customers” tab and then click “Create Segment” in the upper right corner and name the segment “VIPs”.3. Send the broadcast message: this step is a work in progress on our end, so I can help you with this one manually if you give me the message that you’d like to send to all VIPs. However, in the near future, you’ll be able to do this by creating a broadcast message (the location is going to change for this) to a specific segment — in this case, the “VIPs” segment.Let me know the message and everyone on the VIPs (or if you do steps 1 and 2, just the message) and I can fire off the message! Ok awesome thank you so much for this @Jon OBryan. The plan is to send the message Monday AM, so I’ll set this up and get the message over to you today 🙏 @Jon OBryan @Rahul AsatiI’m setting this up rn — do they have to already been imported as a customer (aka had support tickets in the past) in order for me to tag them as VIP? @Annette Sung We sync customer data from Segment so depending on when you application sends data update to segment (mostly likely when they access hotplate application), those customer data will be in Atlas without them ever logging a ticket

Hey @Jon OBryan!I’ve taken a look into one of the problematic emails and it looks like the email has a charset attribute as part of the `Content-Type` header that states the windows-1255 character set which is the windows hebrew charset.Probably the way to fix the issue is to parse this header and use the provided encoding to convert the existing encoding to utf-8. it looks like in JS that can be done using a package called iconv-lite https://github.com/ashtuchkin/iconv-lite which `nodemailer` ’s mailparser uses internally - https://nodemailer.com/extras/mailparser/I hope this helps. Jon -- is there a way to create an email alias where replies are automatically captured in Atlas? I'm thinking of a flow where we send an engagement email (welcome, re-activation, etc.) and the replies can be track and engaged with in Atlas (I like this ticket bot) Another way to achieve the same would be using RoutingYou should be able to configure channels -> email and then, subject or message contains activation.As an action, you should be able to apply tagBased in the tag applied we can create an inbox where all the replies would be gathered under a single inbox.This way you would be able to segregate the replies based on campaign type. Got it @Maxwell Bonnie, yep! If you don’t care about where it is routed, all you have to do is add a sender> for the engagement email address (eg, mailto:welcome@saltbox.com).When you’ve done this, if a customer responds to a transactional email on, eg, mailto:welcome@saltbox.com, it will register as a new ticket in Atlas.--------If you want those tickets to go to a specific inbox, you can do what @Piyush Raj mentioned above (spelled the steps out below with some screenshots as well).To do this:1. To setup a subchannel, you create a new sender in Email config (screenshot 1). 2. Then you can route based off of the subchannel in the Routing rules (screenshot 2). Set the action as “Set tags” and use a tag called “Welcome” (you can create it here). 3. Add a new inbox (screenshot 3) and set the filters for it as Ticket.Tags is Welcome then click “Create inbox” (screenshot 4).When you’ve done this, if a customer responds to a transactional email on, eg, welcome@saltbox.com, it will register as a new ticket in Atlas and send it to the associated inbox.

@Jon OBryan Forgot to ask during our demo yesterday - does Atlas has calling and SMS capabilities? If we were to integrate it with HubSpot, are we able to use those capabilities through HubSpot as well? Let me know, thanks! Hi @Michaela McDermott! You can send SMS natively through us and you can call via integration (eg, Dialpad or Aircall).• For SMS, you just choose the channel as SMS when you’re sending a new message. Anyone who sends an SMS to you will automatically have a ticket created (or, if they have a ticket open, the message will be associated with that ticket). They show up in your timeline like other tickets, • For phone, you will see any calls (in or out) in the customer timeline, and if you have it enabled w/ the telephony tool (Dialpad, Aircall), you can see the call transcript and a link to the call recording. To make a call, you can click on the phone button in the customer’s info pane (from within Atlas).Lmk if any of that is unclear and I can send a Loom or hop on a quick call to clarify.
